title: Streams vs pub/sub vs lists
description: Pub/sub is fire-and-forget, lists are simple queues, and streams add persistence, groups, and replay.
body: Use when choosing a Redis messaging primitive. Pub/sub delivers to currently-connected subscribers only and retains nothing — fine for ephemeral notifications, lossy for work. A list with LPUSH/BRPOP is a simple durable queue but lacks acknowledgements and consumer groups, so an entry popped by a crashed worker is lost. Streams persist entries, support consumer groups with per-entry acks (XACK), redelivery of unacked entries (XPENDING/XCLAIM), and replay from any id. Do not use pub/sub or a bare list for at-least-once work. Actions: pick streams when loss is unacceptable. Validation signals include an offline consumer still receiving backlogged stream entries. Risk boundary: a list-based queue without reclaim loses in-flight work on a worker crash.
